channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Trump tells nations affected by Middle East blockade to secure their own oil
item.description: US President Donald Trump don yan say any country wey never join di Middle East wahala but dey face fuel wahala suppose find their own oil for di Strait of Hormuz. For one post for him Truth Social ac...

item.title: Concentration of Vape Shops in Cheetham Hill, Manchester, and Its Impact on the Industry
item.description: Cheetham Hill in Manchester has become an unexpected hub for the UK vaping industry, hosting over 50 specialised vape shops on two adjacent backstreets near the former Strangeways prison. Many of thes...

item.title: Dar Mayor Recognizes Platforms Supporting Tanzanian Creative Talent
item.description: Dar es Salaam Mayor Nurdin Bilal Juma, popularly known as 'Shetta', has highlighted the importance of platforms like Her Creative Spark in supporting and developing young creative talent in Tanzania. ...

item.title: Dar City aim for quarterfinals in decisive BAL Kalahari Conference game
item.description: Dar City from Tanzania dey ready for one important BAL match against Libya's Al Ahly Ly today at SunBet Arena in Pretoria, South Africa. Dis game go determine whether Dar City go advance to the quarte...

item.title: Mamata Banerjee alleges voter enrolment irregularities ahead of Bengal elections
item.description: West Bengal chief minister Mamata Banerjee has written to the Chief Election Commissioner, Gyanesh Kumar, alleging large-scale attempts by the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) to enrol outsiders as voters...

item.title: Victim Calls for Accountability as Statutory Grooming Gangs Inquiry is Launched
item.description: A new statutory independent inquiry into grooming gangs in England and Wales has been launched following revelations from survivors of child sexual exploitation. One victim, known only as Penny, recou...

item.title: Ol Kalou MP Kiaraho to be buried in Tigoni, sparking mixed reactions
item.description: Residents of Ol Kalou are still coming to terms with the news that their late MP, David Njuguna Kiaraho, chose to be buried outside the constituency he served since 2013. His burial will take place at...

item.title: El-Rufai Appears Again In Kaduna Court As His Trial Resumes After His Mother’s Burial
item.description: Former Kaduna State Governor, Nasir El-Rufai, show face for Kaduna State High Court on Tuesday morning to continue him trial wey concern alleged money laundering and financial wahala. Dem see am waka ...

item.title: Masai Ujiri becomes part-owner of WNBA’s Toronto Tempo
item.description: Masai Ujiri, the former president of the Toronto Raptors, has joined the ownership group of the WNBA team Toronto Tempo. Ujiri spent 12 years with the Raptors, rising to the position of team president...

item.title: Alberta Conservatives Defend Patriotism Amid Separatist Petition Controversy
item.description: In Alberta, political tensions rose as the United Conservative Party (UCP) faced criticism following support by MLA Jason Stephan for a petition calling for a referendum on Alberta separatism. Premier...

item.title: Major Road Upgrades Begin in Dar es Salaam Under DMDPP II Project
item.description: DAR ES SALAAM – The second phase of the Dar es Salaam Metropolitan Development Project (DMDPP II) is now in full swing, with a massive 56-kilometre road upgrade taking place in Ilala Municipality. The...

item.title: Punjab BJP delegation discusses border area concerns with Amit Shah in Delhi
item.description: A delegation of the Punjab BJP visited Delhi to meet with Union Home Minister Amit Shah. The delegation highlighted several concerns related to the border regions of Punjab, focusing on security issue...

item.title: Unable to Access NDTV Education Page for School Assembly News on March 31
item.description: The page from NDTV Education regarding school assembly news headlines for March 31 could not be accessed. Users attempting to visit the link encountered an access denial message indicating that the se...

item.title: Oga Obinna Shares Special Breakfast with Son after 1st Term Ends at Nairobi School
item.description: Kenyan YouTuber Oga Obinna recently created a heartwarming moment with his son, Luirrone, after picking him up from Nairobi School at the close of the first term. The father-son duo enjoyed quality ti...

item.title: Government reviews public institutions’ financial performance and accountability
item.description: Dar es Salaam – President Samia Suluhu Hassan has received three major oversight reports for the 2024/25 financial year from the Controller and Auditor General (CAG), the Prevention and Combating of C...

item.title: Court orders Meesha Shafi to pay damages to Ali Zafar in defamation case
item.description: A Lahore sessions court has ordered singer Meesha Shafi to pay Rs5 million to fellow artist Ali Zafar in a defamation case. The decision, issued on Tuesday, follows years of legal proceedings that beg...

item.title: How to Avoid Airport Mix-Ups When Booking Flights
item.description: When booking flights, it’s important to pay attention to airport codes to avoid unexpected inconveniences or extra costs. Many cities have multiple airports, and budget airlines often use secondary ai...

item.title: Avi Lewis Wins NDP Leadership and Challenges Liberal Party
item.description: The federal NDP has taken a sharp turn to the left with Avi Lewis's decisive first-ballot victory in the party's leadership race. With the NDP currently holding minimal influence in the House of Commo...

item.title: Israel enacts death penalty law targeting Palestinians convicted of deadly attacks
item.description: On Monday, Israel's parliament passed a controversial law making the death penalty by hanging the default sentence for Palestinians convicted in military courts of deadly attacks. Critics highlight th...

item.title: Lahore Court Orders Meesha Shafi to Pay Rs5 Million to Ali Zafar in Defamation Case
item.description: In a significant legal development, a sessions court in Lahore has directed singer Meesha Shafi to pay Rs5 million to fellow singer and actor Ali Zafar in a defamation case. The case, which dates back...
